subject: Chemistry
title: Gay-Lussac's Law of Combining Volumes
description: One-minute skill: Gay-Lussac's Law of Combining Volumes.

hook.text: Joseph Gay-Lussac discovered that gases combine in simple whole-number volume ratios. This puzzled everyone.

buildup.text: Two volumes of hydrogen combine with one volume of oxygen to make two volumes of water vapor. Always 2:1:2.

discovery.text: Simple ratios suggested that gases contain equal numbers of particles in equal volumes—but Dalton disagreed.

twist.text: Dalton couldn't accept that some elements existed as two-atom molecules (H₂, O₂). He thought atoms were always single.

climax.text: Avogadro solved it: gases form diatomic molecules. Two hydrogen molecules plus one oxygen molecule makes two water molecules.

punchline.text: Simple volume ratios, diatomic molecules. Gases were counting atoms for us.

quiz.question: What did Gay-Lussac's law reveal about how gases combine?
options: They react in simple whole-number volume ratios
Heavier gases always sink below lighter gases
